req.title: Account-level transaction limits (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has four testable concerns: (1) a valid limit submission within regulatory caps returns 202/pending status, (2) a duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original response without side-effects, (3) a limit at exactly the regulatory cap boundary is accepted, (4) a limit one unit above the cap is rejected with 422. State transitions cover: unauthenticated → authenticated → limit-pending → limit-active (after cool-down). Tests are scoped to the API layer and use a shared auth fixture plus a deterministic idempotency-key generator.

tests.name: test_set_valid_daily_limit_within_cap_returns_pending
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Requested limit is within the regulatory cap (e.g., 5000 < CAP)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains effective_after timestamp ~2 hours in the future
trace: R-FIN-092: pending limit stored on valid submission
R-FIN-092: changes take effect after 2-hour cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_set_valid_daily_limit_within_cap_returns_pending(
    auth_client,       # <fixture>: authenticated httpx.Client with base_url
    account_id,        # <fixture>: valid account ID for the test customer
    regulatory_cap,    # <fixture>: int, e.g. 10_000
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": regulatory_cap - 1}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = auth_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    effective_after = datetime.fromisoformat(body["effective_after"])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    assert effective_after >= now + timedelta(hours=1, minutes=55)
    assert effective_after <= now + timedelta(hours=2, minutes=5)

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
A prior successful limit submission was made with a known Idempotency-Key
actions: Submit POST /accounts/{id}/limits with a fixed Idempotency-Key (first call)
Record the full response body and headers from the first call
Re-submit the identical POST request with the same Idempotency-Key (second call)
expected: Second response body is identical to first response body
No duplicate limit record is created (idempotent state)
trace: R-FIN-092: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response(
    auth_client,
    account_id,
    regulatory_cap,
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"channel": "mobile", "daily_limit": regulatory_cap - 500}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp1 = auth_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp1.status_code == 202
    body1 = resp1.json()

    resp2 = auth_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp2.status_code == 202
    body2 = resp2.json()

    assert body1["limit_id"] == body2["limit_id"]
    assert body1["status"] == body2["status"]
    assert body1["effective_after"] == body2["effective_after"]

tests.name: test_limit_at_exact_regulatory_cap_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/limits with daily_limit equal to exactly the regulatory cap
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No 422 error is returned (cap boundary is inclusive)
trace: R-FIN-092: customer shall configure limits within regulatory caps (boundary inclusive)
R-FIN-092: 422 only when over regulatory cap

tests.name: test_limit_one_unit_above_regulatory_cap_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating regulatory cap violation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_limit_one_unit_above_regulatory_cap_returns_422(
    auth_client,
    account_id,
    regulatory_cap,
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    over_cap = regulatory_cap + 1
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": over_cap}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = auth_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["cap", "limit", "exceed", "regulatory"])

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: No valid JWT is present (unauthenticated client)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains authentication error message
trace: DT: unauthenticated row → reject without processing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
    anon_client,    # <fixture>: httpx.Client with base_url, NO auth headers
    account_id,
    regulatory_cap,
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": regulatory_cap - 1}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = anon_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

tests.name: test_pending_limit_not_active_before_cooldown_elapses
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A limit submission has just been accepted (status=pending)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/limits to create a pending limit
Immediately GET /accounts/{id}/limits to read current active limit
Assert that the newly submitted limit is NOT yet active
expected: GET shows old active limit unchanged (cool-down not yet elapsed)
New limit is listed as pending, not active
trace: R-FIN-092: changes take effect after 2-hour cool-down
STC: pending state must not transition to active before cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_pending_limit_not_active_before_cooldown_elapses(
    auth_client,
    account_id,
    regulatory_cap,
    get_active_limit,   # <helper_fn>(client, account_id) -> dict
):
    baseline = get_active_limit(auth_client, account_id)

    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    new_limit_value = (baseline.get("daily_limit", 1000) + 100)
    if new_limit_value > regulatory_cap:
        pytest.skip("Cannot form a distinct new limit within cap")

    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": new_limit_value}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    post_resp = auth_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 202
    assert post_resp.json()["status"] == "pending"

    current_active = get_active_limit(auth_client, account_id)
    assert current_active.get("daily_limit") == baseline.get("daily_limit"), (
        "Active limit changed before cool-down elapsed"
    )
